title: Allosorus × kurdicus
taxonomy: Allosorus × kurdicus is a hybrid species within the fern family. It is classified as a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Allosorus. Allosorus × kurdicus is further classified as part of the Pteridophyta division and the Polypodiales order. This species is also known to be part of the Aspleniaceae family. The exact classification of Allosorus × kurdicus is still being researched and refined by botanists.

intro: Allosorus × kurdicus, also known as Kurdish Psyllium or Kurdish Plantago, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the Plantaginaceae family, characterized by its hairy leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, due to its prebiotic properties, which can help regulate gut bacteria and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The bioactive constituents of Allosorus × kurdicus include mucilages, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Allosorus × kurdicus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs, to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of constipation and diarrhea.
